summary: The Docker-based reproduction on Windows demonstrates that Poetry's execute function uses shell=True without sanitization, allowing shell metacharacter injection through 'poetry run' arguments. A malicious command with the & metacharacter successfully chains commands and writes an arbitrary file outside the intended command execution, confirming the command injection vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: The injected shell metacharacters should cause cmd.exe to interpret the & operator and execute a second command that writes a proof file to C:\Windows\Temp\pwned.txt, demonstrating arbitrary command execution beyond the intended 'echo test' command.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh on a Windows container built the image with Python 3.10 and Poetry 2.3.2, created a test project, executed 'poetry run echo test & echo EVOHUNT_SHELL_INJECTION_SUCCESS > C:\Windows\Temp\pwned.txt', and successfully created the proof file at C:\Windows\Temp\pwned.txt through the shell injection, confirming the vulnerability.
verification_method: The verify.sh script uses PowerShell to test the shell injection by running 'poetry run' with a command containing the & metacharacter. The & character chains two commands in cmd.exe: the first is 'echo test' and the second writes a proof file. If the proof file exists at C:\Windows\Temp\pwned.txt, it confirms the shell injection succeeded and arbitrary commands can be executed.
evidence_produced_by_run: Windows container with Python 3.10 and Poetry 2.3.2 successfully built
Test project created with minimal pyproject.toml
Command 'echo test & echo EVOHUNT_SHELL_INJECTION_SUCCESS > C:\Windows\Temp\pwned.txt' injected through poetry run
Shell metacharacter & interpreted by cmd.exe due to shell=True
Proof file C:\Windows\Temp\pwned.txt successfully created through command injection
Content of proof file confirms arbitrary command execution
limitations_or_blockers: Vulnerability only affects Windows systems where Poetry uses shell=True in execute()
Linux and macOS use os.execvpe() instead, which is not vulnerable
Requires attacker to control arguments to 'poetry run' through malicious pyproject.toml, CI/CD, or local access
Cannot use Docker-based PoC on macOS/Linux host without Windows containers support

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
